capabilities.description: Formalised analysis of how assets fail and which failures matter, used to pick the right maintenance strategy (run-to-fail vs PM vs predictive) per failure mode rather than per asset.

capabilities.name: Spare Parts Optimisation
capabilities.description: Analytical layer on top of spare-parts inventory: what to stock, in what quantity, where — balancing stock-out risk against carrying cost and obsolescence.

requirements.text: Determine optimal safety stock levels based on demand variability and supplier lead times

proxy_refs: Stock-out cost modelling (production loss + penalty + premium shipping)

proxy_refs: Obsolescence tracking (slow-moving and dead-stock identification)

requirements.text: Track supersession and alternate-part relationships to optimize stock consolidation and reduce redundant inventory

requirements.text: Perform what-if scenario analysis to evaluate the impact of stocking-policy changes on cost and service levels
proxy_refs: What-if analysis for stocking-policy changes

capabilities.name: Disconnected / Offline Field Operations
capabilities.description: Beyond basic mobile — full workflows for technicians operating in remote locations with no connectivity, including multi-day disconnection and sync conflict resolution.

requirements.text: Enable full offline operation for extended periods (hours to days) without degraded user experience or loss of functionality
proxy_refs: Full offline operation for hours or days without degraded UX

proxy_refs: Bulk download of relevant assets and work orders before going offline

requirements.text: Detect and resolve data conflicts automatically or with user guidance when syncing after offline work
proxy_refs: Local conflict detection and resolution on sync

requirements.text: Prioritize sync of critical data when bandwidth is constrained or connectivity is intermittent
proxy_refs: Priority-based sync when bandwidth is limited

requirements.text: Queue attachments (photos, videos, documents) locally and defer upload until connectivity is available

requirements.text: Support barcode and QR code scanning in offline mode

capabilities.name: Asset Leasing & Lease Accounting
capabilities.description: Handle assets that are leased in (the organisation is the lessee) or leased out (the organisation is the lessor), including compliance with IFRS 16 / ASC 842 lease accounting standards which require even operating leases to be capitalised.

requirements.text: Maintain a lease contract register capturing lessor/lessee identity, lease commencement and end dates, renewal terms, and other key contract terms.
proxy_refs: Lease contract register with lessor / lessee / start / end / renewal terms

requirements.text: Automatically calculate right-of-use (ROU) asset and lease liability on lease commencement in accordance with IFRS 16 / ASC 842.
proxy_refs: Right-of-use (ROU) asset and lease liability auto-calculation (IFRS 16 / ASC 842)

requirements.text: Automatically reclassify leases between operating and finance lease classifications when lease criteria change.
proxy_refs: Automatic reclassification between operating and finance lease on criteria change

requirements.text: Support lease payment schedules with indexed or stepped payment amounts over the lease term.
proxy_refs: Lease payment schedule with indexed or stepped payments

requirements.text: Handle lease modifications (changes to scope or term) with re-measurement of ROU asset and lease liability.
proxy_refs: Lease modification handling (scope or term change) with re-measurement

requirements.text: Track residual value and manage end-of-lease workflows including asset return or purchase option execution.
proxy_refs: Residual value tracking and end-of-lease return / purchase option workflow

requirements.text: Support lessor revenue recognition for assets leased out to third parties.
proxy_refs: Lessor revenue recognition for leased-out assets

requirements.text: Identify and account for embedded leases within service contracts to ensure IFRS 16 compliance.
proxy_refs: Embedded lease identification in service contracts (IFRS 16 compliance)

capabilities.name: OT / ICS Cybersecurity & Asset Vulnerability
capabilities.description: Operational-technology security tied to the asset register: patch status, known vulnerabilities, and incidents per connected asset (PLCs, SCADA, ICS gateways, industrial IoT). Distinct from IT cybersecurity because the threat model prioritises availability and safety over confidentiality.

requirements.text: Integrate CVE feeds and map known vulnerabilities to each asset

requirements.text: Map OT network zones and conduits (per IEC 62443) to asset locations

requirements.text: Maintain security incident history per asset with integration to IT security tools

requirements.text: Enforce lockout and approval workflow for patch and firmware changes

requirements.text: Register and track compensating controls for assets that cannot be patched
proxy_refs: Compensating-control register for assets that cannot be patched

requirements.text: Calculate risk score per asset combining criticality, vulnerability exposure, and network exposure
proxy_refs: Risk score per asset combining criticality × vulnerability × exposure

requirements.text: Maintain audit trail of all remote-access sessions to OT assets
proxy_refs: Audit trail of all remote-access sessions to OT assets
